info.title: zerotrust API
info.description: Zero-trust authentication and authorization API. Built on PASETO v4, ABAC/RBAC, device attestation, and continuous access evaluation.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Developer API key listing, creation, and revocation.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Administrative settings, user management, audit visibility, and privileged operator actions.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Core account lifecycle: registration, login, token refresh, password reset, account linking, and user profile access.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Stripe-backed subscriptions, checkout, portal, usage, pricing, tax, and VAT endpoints.

tags.name: Compliance
tags.description: SOC 2 readiness, controls, and risk-assessment workflows.

tags.name: Feedback
tags.description: Authenticated product feedback submission.

tags.name: GDPR
tags.description: User privacy export, account deletion, and deletion-cancel endpoints.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Operational health and readiness endpoints used by monitors, load balancers, and deployments.

tags.name: MFA
tags.description: Multi-factor authentication setup and verification, including TOTP, Email OTP, and backup codes.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: In-app notifications, unread counts, preferences, SSE, and web-push subscriptions.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Social sign-in and provider callback flows for Google, GitHub, and Facebook.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Workspace, team, invitation, membership, and organization role-management endpoints.

tags.name: Passkeys
tags.description: WebAuthn / FIDO2 passkey registration and authentication flows.

tags.name: Password Reset
tags.description: Password reset request, verification, and completion endpoints.

tags.name: Regions
tags.description: Custom domain resolution, region health, geo-routing, branding, domains, and data residency.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Full-text and ranked smart search plus indexing operations.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Authenticated session listing, revocation, and device/session lifecycle controls.

tags.name: Shared Signals
tags.description: SSF security event receiver endpoints for cross-system identity and risk signals.

tags.name: Support
tags.description: Support ticket and message workflows.

tags.name: Unsubscribe
tags.description: Email unsubscribe landing (API/SDK-only). GET /auth/unsubscribe serves server-rendered HTML for one-click email opt-out links — no Next.js UI page.

tags.name: Wallet
tags.description: Wallet balance, transactions, and top-up. POST /wallet/spend is API/SDK-only (programmatic debit for integrations — no dashboard UI).

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Outbound webhook endpoint registration, delivery history, and provider event receivers.

delete.summary: Revoke an approved JIT grant (admin only)

post.summary: Approve a JIT grant request (admin only)
Deny a JIT grant request (admin only)

delete.summary: Revoke a specific session by ID (admin only)

patch.summary: Update user status, roles, or displayName (admin only)

delete.summary: Soft-delete user and revoke all sessions (admin only)

get.summary: List all sessions for a user (admin only)

delete.summary: Revoke all sessions for a user (admin only)

post.summary: Revoke all sessions for the authenticated user
Redeem a backup code for authentication

target.description: Email address that should receive the OTP

400.description: Invalid code or TOTP not enabled

post.summary: Initialize TOTP setup — returns secret and QR code

qrDataUrl.description: Data URI for QR code image

post.summary: Verify TOTP code and activate TOTP MFA

200.description: TOTP activated — backup codes returned once

get.summary: Begin OAuth authorization flow (returns the provider's authorize URL)
get.description: Generates a CSRF state and (where supported) a server-side PKCE pair, then returns the provider authorization URL the client should navigate to. The PKCE code_verifier never leaves the server.

Provider redirects here with an authorization code. On success the server creates a session, stores the tokens under a one-time exchange code, and 302-redirects to the frontend at /login?oauth_code=<code>. The SPA redeems it via POST /auth/oauth/exchange.

302.description: Redirect to the frontend with a one-time oauth_code (success) or an error query param

400.description: Missing code, invalid/expired state, or PKCE failure

post.summary: Redeem a one-time OAuth exchange code for tokens
post.description: Exchanges the single-use code delivered to /login?oauth_code=<code> for the access and refresh tokens. Keeps tokens out of the URL/history.

post.summary: Generate an ephemeral OAuth state token (PKCE/nonce)

200.description: Reset code sent (response is identical whether email exists or not)

password.description: Must include uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character

post.summary: Rotate refresh token and issue new access token

get.description: API/SDK-only. Verifies a signed token from email links and returns an HTML confirmation page (not JSON). No dashboard UI — email templates link directly to this API route.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Signed unsubscribe token from the email link

400.description: Invalid or missing token (HTML error page)

get.summary: List organizations the current user belongs to

delete.summary: Remove a member (cannot remove the last owner)

get.summary: Get the org security policy (session/device/geo limits)

delete.summary: Decline (delete) one of the caller's own pending invites

404.description: Invite not found or not addressed to the caller

post.summary: Accept a pending org invite by token

403.description: Invite was sent to a different email address

404.description: Invite not found or already used

get.summary: List the authenticated user's pending org invites

200.description: Array of pending invites (with org name/slug) matching the caller's email

get.summary: List active sessions for authenticated user

post.summary: Receive a Security Event Token (SET) from a provider

post.description: Programmatic wallet debit for integrations and background jobs. Not exposed in the dashboard UI — use the API or generated SDK from server-side code with a user or service token.
